hours_formula_detail: New Jersey uses the flat federal baseline of 80 hours/month (averaging 20 hours/week) for ALL qualifying ABAWD work activities, including general nonprofit volunteering. The NJ DHS NJ SNAP ABAWD page (nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/abawd.shtml) lists the threshold as 'Working at least 80 hours a month, or an average of 20 hours a week' and explicitly defines work as 'employment, self-employment, volunteer work, or in-kind work (working in exchange for goods and services instead of cash).' N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.20(a) codifies the same 20-hours-per-week (averaged monthly) standard. There is NO SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula applied to general nonprofit volunteering in New Jersey — that formula exists only in the separate federal workfare lane (7 CFR 273.7(m)), which is the 'Workfare program for the number of hours assigned each month' lane mentioned separately on the NJ SNAP page. A recipient may self-initiate volunteering at any nonprofit; the NJ SNAP page does not impose an MOU, site-registration, or county pre-approval requirement.
enforced_2026_detail: New Jersey enforces the ABAWD 3-month time limit statewide effective February 1, 2026, EXCEPT for two retained waivered jurisdictions: Camden City and Cape May County. Per the NJ DHS Division of Family Development 'Federal Changes' page (nj.gov/humanservices/dfd/news/federal-changes/), 'New Jersey was under a waiver for all counties except Morris through January 31, 2026. As of February 1, 2026, only Camden City and Cape May County are under a time limit waiver.' Under H.R. 1 of 2025 (One Big Beautiful Bill Act, Public Law 119-21, signed July 4, 2025), the ABAWD age range is expanded to 18-64 (NJ DFD page notes the federal change as '18 to 65' for the 80-hours/month mandate; FNS implementation memos confirm 18-64 with effect through the month containing the 64th birthday). The parent/caregiver exemption now applies only when the youngest child in the household is under 14 (previously under 18). Veterans, individuals experiencing homelessness, and former foster youth under age 25 lost their categorical exceptions; new exceptions were added for individuals recognized as Indian / Urban Indian / California Indian under the Indian Health Care Improvement Act. DFD reports it 'continues to apply for federal ABAWD waivers in eligible areas' and is 'in communication with the Department of State, Office of Volunteerism to expand volunteer opportunities for ABAWDs.'
minimum_wage_note: Set to 0 because New Jersey's general volunteer lane is federal_80 and does not use a wage-based divisor. For reference only: the New Jersey statewide minimum wage effective January 1, 2026 is $15.49/hour for most employers (NJ Department of Labor & Workforce Development press release dated October 1, 2025 — the rate was announced as the 2026 figure and confirmed by NJDOL benefit-rate notices; a separate seasonal/small-employer tier sets $14.53→$15.23). Note: some commentary references a forward $15.92 rate, but that figure is the NJDOL-announced rate for January 1, 2027 indexing, not 2026. The minimum wage is annually CPI-indexed under P.L. 2019, c.32. Federal minimum wage of $7.25 appears in NJ's manual only as a wage-based threshold for the separate Work Registration exemption (e.g., 30 hrs/week or $217.50/week), not as a divisor against SNAP allotments for volunteer hour computation.

regional_wages.label: New Jersey statewide minimum wage 2026 (reference only; NOT used in the volunteer-hour calculation)

qualifying_org_definition: Any nonprofit / qualifying organization. The NJ SNAP ABAWD page describes acceptable activity simply as 'volunteer work' alongside paid employment, self-employment, in-kind work, SNAP E&T, and workfare. The page does not impose a statewide approved-site list, an MOU with the County Social Service Agency (CSSA), or a county pre-approval gate for volunteer hours. NJ DFD reports it is 'in communication with the Department of State, Office of Volunteerism to expand volunteer opportunities for ABAWDs' — indicating the agency treats general volunteerism (not pre-vetted state programs only) as the operative lane. N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.20 codifies the work-activity standard without limiting the host organization type beyond the federal default.
verification_instrument: No NJ-specific statewide volunteer-verification form (analogous to CA's CF 888 or MD's FIA 500-B) is named in publicly available NJ DHS/DFD primary sources reviewed. The NJ SNAP ABAWD page states only: 'In order to show you're meeting the ABAWD work requirements, you'll need to provide documents that prove you're working, volunteering, or participating in an approved program for the equivalent of 80 hours a month.' Reporting is to the County Social Service Agency (CSSA, sometimes called the County Welfare Agency / CWA) by online portal, phone, mail, or in-person. NJ DFD references 'screening tools' and a 'new screening notice' developed for CSSA workers post-HR1, but these are exemption-screening instruments, not volunteer-hour verification forms. Practically, verification is documented via supervisor letter / signed attestation collected by the CSSA caseworker at certification, redetermination, or interim change. Coded as 'silent' on a named statewide form pending discovery of an internal DFD instruction memo (e.g., a numbered NJ DFD Instruction or DFD-IM) that designates a specific form number.
submission_mechanism_detail: Per the NJ SNAP ABAWD page, recipients 'can submit your changes online at www.MyNJHelps.gov or to your County Social Service Agency.' Additional channels cited on NJ SNAP pages: phone, mail, or in-person at the CSSA office. MyNJHelps.gov is the statewide online portal for SNAP, TANF, and Medicaid changes; a companion MyNJHelps mobile app is published by NJ DHS. SNAP is administered locally in NJ by the 21 County Social Service Agencies (also called County Welfare Agencies / CWAs or County Boards of Social Services, e.g., Burlington County Board of Social Services). NJ does not operate a single state-run district office system; intake, verification review, and exemption screening happen at the county level.
submission_deadline: Mid-period 10-day reporting trigger: per the NJ SNAP ABAWD page, 'If you are subject to the ABAWD Time Limit Rules and your work hours drop below 80 hours a month, you must let your County Social Service Agency know within 10 days.' Beyond that interim-change trigger, verification flows through the standard SNAP simplified-reporting cadence (at initial certification, at recertification, and on interim change). No fixed monthly filing date is published; the operative rule is the 10-day change-report obligation plus periodic recertification.
remote_stance_detail: No NJ DHS/DFD primary source reviewed (the NJ SNAP ABAWD page, the DFD Federal Changes page, or N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.20) addresses whether remote / online / virtual volunteer hours qualify toward the ABAWD requirement. Federal rule 7 CFR §273.24 does not categorically exclude remote volunteering. Coded 'silent' because NJ policy is neither affirmative nor restrictive on this point. This is a product-guidance gap, not a classification factor.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No NJ DHS/DFD source reviewed restricts the host nonprofit to New Jersey-based entities. The NJ SNAP ABAWD page describes qualifying volunteer activity only by type ('volunteer work'), with no in-state-location requirement. N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.20 likewise does not impose a host-organization geographic limit. Federal default under 7 CFR 273.24 accepts any qualifying nonprofit regardless of location; absent an explicit NJ restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally acceptable. Coded 'silent' because no source affirmatively permits or forbids it.
required_prestep_detail: No EDP (Employment Development Plan), work-registration gate specific to the volunteer lane, CSSA case-manager assignment, MOU, or county site pre-approval is required for general nonprofit volunteering. Work Registration (N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.1) and the ABAWD work participation requirement (10:87-10.20) are separate rule sets; a recipient may self-initiate volunteering and present documentation at certification/redetermination/interim change. Eligibility workers may make NJ SNAP E&T referrals when the ABAWD requests one, but no referral is required to begin counting volunteer hours. DFD's post-HR1 'screening notice' is an exemption-screening instrument (sent to identify potential exemptions), not a participation prerequisite.
geographic_waivers: Camden City and Cape May County hold ABAWD time-limit waivers effective February 1, 2026, per the NJ DHS DFD Federal Changes page: 'As of February 1, 2026, only Camden City and Cape May County are under a time limit waiver.' All other 20 New Jersey counties (Atlantic, Bergen, Burlington, Camden county outside the city, Cape May-Atlantic was already separately treated, Cumberland, Essex, Gloucester, Hudson, Hunterdon, Mercer, Middlesex, Monmouth, Morris, Ocean, Passaic, Salem, Somerset, Sussex, Union, Warren) are subject to enforcement. Morris County was the lone non-waivered county under the prior statewide-minus-Morris waiver that ran through January 31, 2026. DFD reports it 'continues to apply for federal ABAWD waivers in eligible areas' under the narrower HR1 waiver criteria (≥10% local unemployment).
county_variation_detail: NJ SNAP is administered locally by 21 County Social Service Agencies (CSSAs / CWAs / County Boards of Social Services). Statewide policy is uniform under N.J.A.C. 10:87 and DFD-issued instructions, but the ABAWD time limit applies non-uniformly because of the partial geographic waiver: Camden City and Cape May County recipients are not subject to the 3-month time limit, while the remaining jurisdictions are. Operationally, all volunteer-hour documentation flows through the recipient's CSSA, which may apply local intake practices (paper packet vs. portal upload), though core policy is statewide.
unresolved: verification_form_name: no named NJ statewide volunteer-verification form was located in publicly available NJ DHS/DFD sources; NJ may rely on generic supervisor-letter / case-worker-collected documentation, or a CSSA-internal form that is not published online. Confirming via a DFD Instruction (DFD-IM) or directly with a county CSSA would resolve this.
remote_stance: no NJ source confirms whether remote/online/virtual volunteer hours count.
accepts_outofstate_nonprofit: no NJ source affirmatively permits or forbids an out-of-state 501(c)(3) signing the volunteer verification; coded 'silent' on federal-default reasoning only.
Discretionary Exemption volume for FFY2026 in NJ is not published in the sources reviewed; DFD references general use of exemptions but does not state an FFY2026 allocation.
form_required_fields list is reconstructed from federal-default + parallel-state norms (CA CF 888, MD FIA 500-B, PA-1938), not from an NJ-published form.
notes: VERIFICATION (2026-06-23, FY2026 / OBBBA-HR1): New Jersey is a federal_80 state with a PARTIAL geographic waiver. The decisive primary source for the volunteer lane is the NJ SNAP ABAWD page (nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/abawd.shtml), which states the threshold as '80 hours a month, or an average of 20 hours a week' and defines qualifying work to include 'volunteer work.' N.J.A.C. 10:87-10.20(a) codifies the same standard. NJ DHS DFD's Federal Changes page documents the HR1 transition: prior statewide-minus-Morris waiver expired January 31, 2026; from February 1, 2026 forward only Camden City and Cape May County retain ABAWD time-limit waivers. HR1 (P.L. 119-21, July 4, 2025) expanded the age range to 18-64, lowered the parent/caregiver shielded-child age to under 14, removed the veteran/homeless/former-foster-youth exceptions, and added Indian/Urban Indian/California Indian exceptions. Administration: SNAP intake and verification flow through 21 County Social Service Agencies (CSSAs / County Welfare Agencies). Submission channels: MyNJHelps.gov online portal, MyNJHelps mobile app, phone, mail, or in-person at the CSSA. Verification form: NJ DOES NOT publish a named statewide volunteer-verification form analogous to CA's CF 888 or MD's FIA 500-B in sources reviewed; documentation appears to be generic supervisor attestation collected by the CSSA. Cadence: 10-day report on interim change (hours dropping below 80) + at certification/redetermination. Remote-volunteer stance and out-of-state nonprofit acceptance are silent in NJ sources. Coded tier 2_partial_waiver (rather than 1_clean) because the Camden-City + Cape-May enforcement carve-out is operationally relevant for any product that routes users by geography. NJ minimum wage 2026 (reference only): $15.49/hour statewide for most employers (NJDOL, January 1, 2026).
key_primary_source_quote: Ways to meet ABAWD work requirements: Working at least 80 hours a month, or an average of 20 hours a week. Work means employment, self-employment, volunteer work, or in-kind work (working in exchange for goods and services instead of cash). [...] In order to show you're meeting the ABAWD work requirements, you'll need to provide documents that prove you're working, volunteering, or participating in an approved program for the equivalent of 80 hours a month. [...] If you are subject to the ABAWD Time Limit Rules and your work hours drop below 80 hours a month, you must let your County Social Service Agency know within 10 days. You can submit your changes online at www.MyNJHelps.gov or to your County Social Service Agency. (NJ SNAP | Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents, nj.gov/humanservices/njsnap/apply/eligibility/abawd.shtml)
